choices.text: <0.3 incompressible (Bernoulli), 0.3-0.8 subsonic compressible, 0.8-1.2 transonic (shockwaves form), >1.2 supersonic, >5 hypersonic (severe heating). Physics + materials change at each boundary.
Atmospheric drag dominates. ISS at 400 km: regular reboosts. <200 km: deorbit in months. >700 km: minimal drag, orbits last centuries. Solar activity cycles affect upper-atmosphere density.

questions.explanation: Drag depends on altitude; LEO needs reboost.
